text: You know, normally I'd greet the

reasons, but well, you get the picture.
I mean, credit to Lindo and Jordan on

It's absolutely spectacular. Anyway,
John suffers from Tourette's syndrome,
which is, and I quote, characterized by
sudden, involuntary, and repetitive
movements and sounds known as ticks.
Between 10 and 30% of people with the
condition have ticks that produce
socially unacceptable words such as
swearing, known as coprolia, according
to the Tourette's action charity. So, in
layman's terms, it can make you
uncontrollably blurt out all kinds of
words and phrases that you really
wouldn't want to say, often at the most
inappropriate of moments. There's no
cure and no real way to manage it. So,
as you can probably imagine, it's a
pretty devastating condition to have to
live with. In fact, funnily enough,
there's literally an entire movie
showing how the condition practically
destroyed his life. Host Alan Cumin was
quick to explain the situation
afterwards in an improvised speech that
did a lot to settle things down.
